appliesTo: future synthesis findings, reviewer recommendations, and CP-phase promotion decisions inside the controlled promotion track

rules.rule: Every recommendation must name exactly one primary destination class before drafting begins.
rules.reason: Prevents review-holding or supporting material from being silently treated as active law.

rules.rule: A BASELINE_PATCH may clarify model/runtime law but must not promote draft schemas by implication.
rules.reason: Baseline law has the highest authority and must remain narrow.

rules.rule: A MACHINE_CONTRACT promotion requires an accepted RFC or an existing active baseline/companion basis plus positive and negative examples.
rules.reason: Schemas should execute law; they should not create law alone.

rules.rule: A CONFORMANCE_FIXTURE proves behavior only inside its tested profile and cannot create active semantic law.
rules.reason: Runtime evidence should not overclaim authority.

rules.rule: Review-holding material can supply evidence or candidate text only after its folder status and stale-disposition are recorded.
rules.reason: Prevents stale or contextual materials from bypassing authority order.

rules.rule: A POSTPONED_ITEM must include the risk that blocks promotion and the separate review needed to reopen it.
rules.reason: Avoids implicit future acceptance of held concepts.

rules.rule: Every finding or recommendation must cite at least one active artifact, supporting artifact, reviewed artifact, or mark insufficient evidence.
